title: narcissus × litigiosus var. toletanus
intro: Narcissus × litigiosus var. toletanus, also known as Toletan Narcissus or Toletan Daffodil, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is believed to possess medicinal properties due to its alkaloid and glycoside content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Narcissus × litigiosus var. toletanus can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and infusions, although its use and availability may be limited due to conservation efforts.
